core_mechanism: spontaneous self-organization of topologically protected, particle-like localized field configurations (topological solitons/defects) into a periodic lattice, stabilized by a chirality-inducing broken-inversion-symmetry term at the boundary between a disordered and an ordered phase
brief_reason: The abstract describes topologically stable, particle-like field configurations spontaneously forming a two-dimensional lattice at the border between the disordered (paramagnetic) and ordered (helimagnetic) phases in a system that lacks inversion symmetry, i.e. chirality-stabilized topological defects arranging into crystalline order.
